name: chat
context: Generate text using the OpenAI Chat Completions API with system/user/assistant messages
info: The Chat Completions API is the primary interface for generating text with OpenAI models. You send an array of messages with roles (system, user, assistant) and receive a model-generated completion. Supports GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o1, o3, and other models. Features include streaming via Server-Sent Events, temperature and top_p sampling controls, structured output via JSON Schema, vision (image inputs), and tool/function calling (see [[openai/function-calling]]). The endpoint is `POST /v1/chat/completions`. The official Node.js SDK (`openai`) wraps this with typed methods and automatic retries.

sections.title: Basic usage
sections.body: Create an OpenAI client (reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from env), call `client.chat.completions.create()` with a model and messages array. Each message has `role` (system, user, assistant, tool) and `content`. The response contains `choices[0].message.content` with the generated text. See [[openai/models]] for available models.

sections.title: Streaming
sections.body: Set `stream: true` to receive responses as Server-Sent Events. Iterate with `for await (const chunk of stream)`. Each chunk contains `choices[0].delta.content` with the incremental text. Streaming reduces time-to-first-token and enables real-time UIs. The final chunk has `finish_reason` set.

sections.title: System prompts
sections.body: The system message (role: system) is the first message in the array and defines the model's persona, capabilities, and constraints. Use it to set tone, output format, domain expertise, and guardrails. System prompts persist across the conversation. Multi-turn conversations append user and assistant messages after the system message.

sections.title: Structured output
sections.body: Use `response_format: { type: 'json_schema', json_schema: { name, schema } }` to constrain output to a JSON Schema. The model guarantees valid JSON matching the schema. Useful for data extraction, classification, and structured responses. Alternatively, `{ type: 'json_object' }` ensures valid JSON without schema validation. See also [[openai/function-calling]] for tool-based structured extraction.

sections.title: Vision
sections.body: GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini accept images in the `content` array alongside text. Pass images as URLs or base64 data URIs. Use `{ type: 'image_url', image_url: { url } }` content parts. Supports analysis, OCR, description, and visual reasoning. Image tokens count toward the context window and billing.
